title: Aviation Procedure
preamble: This procedure applies to all aviation activities associated with the owner-led project: commercial air travel; chartered aircraft (fixed and rotary wing); RPAS/drone operations; and management of any owned or operated airfields and helicopter landing sites. It must be read in conjunction with the Flight Safety Foundation Basic Aviation Risk Standard (BARS).
owns: Nominated Manager – Aviation (NM-A) requirements and delegates
Commercial air travel booking requirements (TMC, List 1/2 airlines)
references: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0004 – Training (NM-A training requirements — documented in RTPR-HSE-REG-0003)
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0006 – Fitness for Work (aviation personnel fitness requirements)
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0040 – Management of Change (NM-A/delegate changes via MoC)
referenced_by: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0026 – Vehicles references RTPR-HSE-PRO-0018 for integration of ground transport with aviation

requirements.section_heading: Nominated Manager – Aviation and Risk Assessment
requirements.text: Require appointment of a Nominated Manager – Aviation (NM-A) in writing, with sufficient authority, Flight Safety Foundation MCAR training, RT Group aviation training, at least one delegate, and a Management of Change process for personnel changes.

requirements.section_heading: Nominated Manager – Aviation and Risk Assessment
requirements.text: Require a risk assessment in consultation with experienced aviation personnel before any aviation activity. Require activity-specific risk assessments before new aviation operations. Require existing assessments to be reviewed at least every two years.

requirements.section_heading: Nominated Manager – Aviation and Risk Assessment
requirements.text: Document aviation management arrangements including: NM-A details and delegates; aviation activity types; applicable BARS standards and critical controls; and assurance processes. Review annually or when the risk profile changes materially.

Require all commercial air travel to be booked through the RT designated Travel Management Company on List 1 (unrestricted) approved airlines. List 2 airlines permitted only where a List 1 airline does not service the route. Non-approved airline travel requires Group Aviation Risk review and Managing Director or higher approval.

Require all charter operators to hold current Group approval and BARS registration (comprehensive stream). Where BARS registration does not cover the specific aircraft type or operation, require a documented review by a Group-approved competent aviation specialist. For non-BARS operators, require a BARS gap analysis by an approved specialist. Require long-term contracted operations (greater than 6 months) to be governed by a written contract including BARS compliance clauses.

Require RPAS operators to hold current Group approval, operate per BARS and applicable regulations, hold all required permits, and implement controls to ensure separation with manned aviation and other RPAS.

Define pre-use inspection requirements: battery charge; firmware current; rotor and propeller function. Require exclusion zones during take-off and landing. Prohibit operations during adverse weather exceeding RPAS limitations. Require flight plans covering proximity to powerlines, infrastructure, personnel, and birdlife. Verify and document personnel competency before operations.

Require owned/operated airfields and HLS to meet ICAO Annex 14 standards and national requirements, including: AWOS or trained weather observer; visual slope guidance; qualified responsible personnel; PCO/HLO; bird control; emergency preparedness; and a current management plan covering inspection, calibration, and maintenance.
Require annual review of all owned/operated airfields by a Group-approved competent aviation specialist.

project_completion_guidance: Appoint the NM-A in writing for this project and confirm delegate appointments. Insert the NM-A name, position, training currency, and delegate details into the designated section of this procedure.

Complete the aviation activity inventory for this project: list all aviation activities that will occur (commercial travel, charter, RPAS, airfield/HLS operations) and the applicable BARS standard for each.
For RPAS operations: define the specific RPAS types approved for this project, the flight approval process, and the exclusion zones for this project's site layout.
For any owned/operated airfields or HLS on this project: complete the airfield/HLS management plan and insert or reference it here.

external_sources.summary: Requires appointment of a Nominated Manager – Aviation, risk assessments before aviation activities, approved airline and charter operator requirements, RPAS Group approval, and ICAO Annex 14 compliance for owned airfields and HLS.

Requires pre-use inspection of RPAS, exclusion zones during operations, prohibition of operations in adverse weather, flight planning covering hazards, and documented personnel competency verification.
